parameters.jsCode:
const data = $input.first().json;

const prompt =
`You are writing narration scripts for a 3Blue1Brown-style physics YouTube video.\n` +
`Topic: ${data.topic}\n\n` +
`Write narration for 3 scenes. The total video will be ~60 seconds.\n` +
`Each narration will be recorded by a voice actor. Write naturally spoken sentences only.\n` +
`DO NOT write any code, equations, or technical jargon — just clear spoken English.\n\n` +
`SCENE WORD TARGETS (ElevenLabs speaks at ~2.5 words/second):\n` +
`- Scene 1 (OpeningScene):     55-65 words → ~22-26 seconds\n` +
`- Scene 2 (CoreConceptScene): 70-80 words → ~28-32 seconds\n` +
`- Scene 3 (SummaryScene):     25-30 words → ~10-12 seconds\n\n` +
`Scene 1 narration: Hook the viewer, introduce the topic, mention real-world relevance.\n` +
`Scene 2 narration: Explain the core physics concept step by step. Use simple analogies.\n` +
`Scene 3 narration: Summarize the key takeaway in 1-2 sentences. End memorably.\n\n` +
`Return ONLY a raw JSON object — no markdown, no code fences:\n` +
`{\n` +
`  "scenes": [\n` +
`    {\n` +
`      "scene_number": 1,\n` +
`      "class_name": "OpeningScene",\n` +
`      "narration": "...",\n` +
`      "visual_hint": "brief description of 2-3 key visuals to show (arrows, equations, diagrams)"\n` +
`    },\n` +
`    {\n` +
`      "scene_number": 2,\n` +
`      "class_name": "CoreConceptScene",\n` +
`      "narration": "...",\n` +
`      "visual_hint": "..."\n` +
`    },\n` +
`    {\n` +
`      "scene_number": 3,\n` +
`      "class_name": "SummaryScene",\n` +
`      "narration": "...",\n` +
`      "visual_hint": "..."\n` +
`    }\n` +
`  ]\n` +
`}`;

return [{
  json: {
    ...data,
    gemini_script_request: {
      contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],
      generationConfig: {
        maxOutputTokens: 4096,
        temperature: 0.8,
        responseMimeType: 'application/json',
        thinkingConfig: { thinkingBudget: 0 }
      }
    }
  }
}];

const data = $input.first().json;

// Build per-scene spec with exact audio duration and narration beat analysis
const sceneSpecs = data.scenes.map(s => {
  const dur = s.audio_duration_seconds || 20;
  const words = s.narration.trim().split(/\s+/);
  const wps = (words.length / dur).toFixed(2);

  // Estimate approximate timing of narration quarters (for animation beat alignment)
  const q1 = (dur * 0.25).toFixed(1);
  const q2 = (dur * 0.50).toFixed(1);
  const q3 = (dur * 0.75).toFixed(1);

  // Split narration into quarters to show which words land at which beat
  const quarterLen = Math.floor(words.length / 4);
  const beat1words = words.slice(0, quarterLen).join(' ');
  const beat2words = words.slice(quarterLen, quarterLen * 2).join(' ');
  const beat3words = words.slice(quarterLen * 2, quarterLen * 3).join(' ');
  const beat4words = words.slice(quarterLen * 3).join(' ');

  return [
    `━━━ SCENE ${s.scene_number}: class_name="${s.class_name}" ━━━`,
    `Audio duration: EXACTLY ${dur.toFixed(2)} seconds (${words.length} words @ ${wps} words/sec)`,
    `Visual hint: ${s.visual_hint || 'physics diagram with equations and vectors'}`,
    `Narration: "${s.narration}"`,
    ``,
    `Narration timing breakdown (sync your animations to these beats):`,
    `  0s – ${q1}s  → "${beat1words}"`,
    `  ${q1}s – ${q2}s → "${beat2words}"`,
    `  ${q2}s – ${q3}s → "${beat3words}"`,
    `  ${q3}s – ${dur.toFixed(1)}s → "${beat4words}"`,
    ``,
    `MANDATORY: sum of all run_time + self.wait() in this scene = ${dur.toFixed(2)} seconds`
  ].join('\n');
}).join('\n\n');

const totalDur = data.scenes.reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.audio_duration_seconds || 20), 0);

const prompt =
`You are a world-class physics animator generating Manim Community Edition v0.20.0 code.\n` +
`Topic: ${data.topic}\n` +
`Total video duration: ${totalDur.toFixed(1)} seconds\n\n` +

`THE NARRATION AUDIO IS ALREADY RECORDED. Your Manim animation MUST sync to it perfectly.\n` +
`Each animation must appear ON SCREEN exactly when the narrator SPEAKS about it.\n\n` +

`${sceneSpecs}\n\n` +

`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`RULE 1 — PERFECT TIMING (non-negotiable):\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`For each scene, the TOTAL of every run_time + every self.wait() MUST equal audio_duration seconds.\n` +
`\n` +
`Standard animation durations to use:\n` +
`  Write(text):                            run_time=1.5\n` +
`  Create(shape) / GrowArrow():            run_time=1.5\n` +
`  FadeIn() / FadeOut():                   run_time=0.8\n` +
`  Transform() / TransformMatchingShapes: run_time=2.0\n` +
`  .animate.shift() / .animate.move_to(): run_time=1.5\n` +
`  .animate.set_value() (ValueTracker):   run_time=3.0 (shows smooth change)\n` +
`  Indicate() / Circumscribe():            run_time=1.0\n` +
`  self.wait() to let narration breathe:  1.5–2.5s\n` +
`  self.wait() at scene end:              0.5s\n` +
`\n` +
`SYNC TECHNIQUE: For each narration beat (0s, quarter, half, 3-quarter of scene),\n` +
`start a matching animation so the visual appears AS the narrator speaks about it.\n` +
`\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`RULE 2 — ZERO OVERLAPPING OBJECTS (non-negotiable):\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`Every single object MUST have an explicit position. Never rely on defaults.\n` +
`\n` +
`SCREEN LAYOUT ZONES (use exactly these regions):\n` +
`  TITLE ZONE:      obj.to_edge(UP).shift(DOWN*0.3)         font_size=38 MAX\n` +
`  LEFT DIAGRAM:    obj.move_to(LEFT*3.2 + UP*0.5)\n` +
`  RIGHT DIAGRAM:   obj.move_to(RIGHT*3.2 + UP*0.5)\n` +
`  CENTER EQUATION: obj.move_to(ORIGIN) or obj.move_to(DOWN*0.5)\n` +
`  BOTTOM LABEL:    obj.to_edge(DOWN).shift(UP*0.3)          font_size=28 MAX\n` +
`\n` +
`POSITIONING RULES:\n` +
`  - Stack vertically with buff=0.5: obj2.next_to(obj1, DOWN, buff=0.5)\n` +
`  - Arrow labels: label.next_to(arrow.get_center(), UP, buff=0.3)\n` +
`  - Axes: axes.move_to(ORIGIN) or axes.move_to(RIGHT*2+DOWN*0.5)\n` +
`  - NEVER place more than 1 object at ORIGIN or UP*0 simultaneously\n` +
`\n` +
`CLEARING BETWEEN SECTIONS:\n` +
`  When switching from one diagram/topic to the next within a scene, ALWAYS clear first:\n` +
`  self.play(*[FadeOut(m, run_time=0.8) for m in self.mobjects]); self.wait(0.3)\n` +
`  (Do NOT do this at the very end of scene — just let it cut naturally)\n` +
`\n` +
`ONLY use self.add() for always_redraw lambda objects (ValueTracker dots/labels).\n` +
`ALL other objects must be introduced with self.play().\n` +
`\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`RULE 3 — ADVANCED ANIMATION QUALITY:\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`Produce 3Blue1Brown-quality animations. Use ALL of the following:\n` +
`\n` +
`COLOR CODING (strict standard):\n` +
`  RED / #FF4444:    forces, important labels\n` +
`  BLUE / #4488FF:   velocity, motion vectors\n` +
`  GREEN / #44FF88:  results, final answers\n` +
`  YELLOW / #FFFF44: equations being highlighted\n` +
`  ORANGE / #FF8844: acceleration, energy\n` +
`  WHITE:            general text, titles\n` +
`  GRAY:             background grid, secondary elements\n` +
`\n` +
`REQUIRED ANIMATION TECHNIQUES (use at least 3 per scene):\n` +
`  1. EQUATION BUILD: Show equation letter by letter using TransformMatchingShapes\n` +
`     eq0=MathTex(r"F").move_to(ORIGIN)\n` +
`     eq1=MathTex(r"F =").move_to(ORIGIN)\n` +
`     eq2=MathTex(r"F = ma").move_to(ORIGIN)\n` +
`     self.play(Write(eq0,run_time=1.0))\n` +
`     self.play(TransformMatchingShapes(eq0,eq1,run_time=1.5))\n` +
`     self.play(TransformMatchingShapes(eq1,eq2,run_time=1.5))\n` +
`\n` +
`  2. MOVING OBJECT: Use .animate chain to show physics in action\n` +
`     ball=Circle(radius=0.3,color=BLUE,fill_opacity=0.8).move_to(LEFT*4)\n` +
`     self.play(Create(ball,run_time=0.8))\n` +
`     self.play(ball.animate.move_to(RIGHT*4),run_time=2.0)\n` +
`\n` +
`  3. VECTOR FIELD: Show multiple arrows with color and labels\n` +
`     v1=Arrow(ORIGIN,RIGHT*2+UP*1,color=RED,buff=0)\n` +
`     lbl1=MathTex(r"\\vec{F}",color=RED,font_size=32).next_to(v1.get_end(),UP,buff=0.2)\n` +
`     self.play(GrowArrow(v1,run_time=1.5),Write(lbl1,run_time=1.0))\n` +
`\n` +
`  4. SMOOTH GRAPH: Axes + plot + moving dot with ValueTracker\n` +
`     axes=Axes(x_range=[0,5,1],y_range=[0,10,2],x_length=5,y_length=3)\n` +
`     axes.move_to(RIGHT*2+DOWN*0.5)\n` +
`     graph=axes.plot(lambda x:2*x,color=BLUE,x_range=[0,5])\n` +
`     t=ValueTracker(0)\n` +
`     dot=always_redraw(lambda:Dot(axes.c2p(t.get_value(),2*t.get_value()),color=YELLOW,radius=0.12))\n` +
`     self.play(Create(axes,run_time=1.5),Create(graph,run_time=1.5))\n` +
`     self.add(dot)\n` +
`     self.play(t.animate.set_value(5),run_time=3.0)\n` +
`\n` +
`  5. HIGHLIGHT PULSE: Use Indicate or Circumscribe to draw attention\n` +
`     self.play(Indicate(key_equation,color=YELLOW,scale_factor=1.2,run_time=1.0))\n` +
`     self.play(Circumscribe(result,color=GREEN,run_time=1.5))\n` +
`\n` +
`  6. SPLIT SCREEN: Two diagrams side-by-side with a DashedLine divider\n` +
`     divider=DashedLine(UP*3,DOWN*3,color=GRAY,dash_length=0.2)\n` +
`     self.play(Create(divider,run_time=0.5))\n` +
`     # left content at LEFT*3, right content at RIGHT*3\n` +
`\n` +
`  7. COLOR CHANGE: Use .animate.set_color() to transition object colors\n` +
`     self.play(equation.animate.set_color(GREEN),run_time=1.0)\n` +
`\n` +
`  8. STAGGERED ENTRY with LaggedStart (ALWAYS use this for lists / multi-part diagrams):\n` +
`     lines = VGroup(\n` +
`       Text("Line 1", font_size=30, color=WHITE),\n` +
`       Text("Line 2", font_size=30, color=BLUE),\n` +
`       Text("Line 3", font_size=30, color=GREEN)\n` +
`     ).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.4).move_to(LEFT*2)\n` +
`     self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(l, shift=RIGHT*0.4) for l in lines], lag_ratio=0.4, run_time=2.0))\n` +
`     # For equations: LaggedStart(*[Write(eq) for eq in eq_group], lag_ratio=0.5, run_time=2.5)\n` +
`\n` +
`  9. BACKGROUND GRID — add to EVERY scene for professional depth (MANDATORY):\n` +
`     grid = NumberPlane(\n` +
`       background_line_style={"stroke_color": BLUE_E, "stroke_width": 1, "stroke_opacity": 0.12},\n` +
`       axis_config={"stroke_opacity": 0}\n` +
`     )\n` +
`     self.add(grid)  # add() is OK ONLY for background/non-animated elements\n` +
`\n` +
`  10. HIGHLIGHT BOX — SurroundingRectangle to frame final results:\n` +
`      box = SurroundingRectangle(final_eq, color=YELLOW, buff=0.18, corner_radius=0.1, stroke_width=2)\n` +
`      self.play(Create(box, run_time=1.0))\n` +
`      self.wait(1.5)\n` +
`      self.play(Flash(box, color=YELLOW, flash_radius=0.5, run_time=0.6))\n` +
`\n` +
`  11. BRACE ANNOTATION — label important measurements or dimensions:\n` +
`      brace = Brace(obj, direction=DOWN, color=GRAY)\n` +
`      brace_lbl = brace.get_text("10 meters", font_size=26).set_color(GRAY)\n` +
`      self.play(GrowFromCenter(brace, run_time=0.8), FadeIn(brace_lbl, run_time=0.8))\n` +
`\n` +
`  12. SCENE OPENING — every scene must start with a bold animated title that fades out:\n` +
`      title = Text("Scene Title Here", font_size=40, weight=BOLD, gradient=(BLUE, PURPLE))\n` +
`      title.to_edge(UP).shift(DOWN*0.2)\n` +
`      underline = Line(title.get_left(), title.get_right(), color=BLUE, stroke_width=2)\n` +
`      underline.next_to(title, DOWN, buff=0.1)\n` +
`      self.play(Write(title, run_time=1.2), Create(underline, run_time=0.6))\n` +
`      self.wait(1.0)\n` +
`      # title/underline stay on screen for the full scene (do not fade them out)\n` +
`\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`STRICT MANIM v0.20.0 API RULES:\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`ONLY IMPORT: from manim import *\n` +
`STRING QUOTES: Use SINGLE QUOTES for ALL Python string literals in the code.\n` +
`  CORRECT:  Text('Newton Laws of Motion')\n` +
`  CORRECT:  Text("Newton's Laws")  ← only use double quotes when string contains apostrophe\n` +
`  WRONG:    Text("Newton Laws of Motion")  ← avoid double quotes unnecessarily\n` +
`DICT ARGS: Use dict() function, NEVER brace syntax for keyword dicts passed to Manim:\n` +
`  CORRECT:  background_line_style=dict(stroke_color=BLUE_E, stroke_width=1, stroke_opacity=0.12)\n` +
`  WRONG:    background_line_style={"stroke_color": BLUE_E}  ← double quotes in braces break JSON\n` +
`DO NOT USE: SVGMobject, ImageMobject, ThreeDScene, ThreeDAxes\n` +
`DO NOT USE: NumberTracker → use ValueTracker instead\n` +
`DO NOT USE: axes.get_graph() → use axes.plot(lambda x: ...) instead\n` +
`DO NOT USE: ShowCreation → use Create instead\n` +
`DO NOT USE: self.add() for non-lambda objects\n` +
`MathTex strings: use raw strings r"..." and double-backslash for LaTeX: r"\\frac{a}{b}"\n` +
`Text font_size: max 42 for titles, max 32 for body text, max 26 for labels\n` +
`\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`OUTPUT FORMAT:\n` +
`═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n` +
`Return ONLY a raw JSON object — no markdown fences, no explanation:\n` +
`{\n` +
`  "scenes": [\n` +
`    { "scene_number": 1, "class_name": "OpeningScene",     "code": "from manim import *\\nclass OpeningScene(Scene):\\n    def construct(self):\\n        ..." },\n` +
`    { "scene_number": 2, "class_name": "CoreConceptScene", "code": "..." },\n` +
`    { "scene_number": 3, "class_name": "SummaryScene",     "code": "..." }\n` +
`  ]\n` +
`}\n` +
`ESCAPE RULE: all newlines inside code strings must be \\\\n (backslash-n) within the JSON`;

return [{
  json: {
    ...data,
    gemini_manim_request: {
      contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],
      generationConfig: {
        maxOutputTokens: 32768,
        temperature: 0.7,
        responseMimeType: 'application/json',
        thinkingConfig: { thinkingBudget: 0 }
      }
    }
  }
}];
